reviewer: agent matrix pass (runner + e2e evidenced, revision 2) + off-main-thread composite retrofit (revision 3)

rubric.evidence: conformance (headless no-GPU, WASM): loads-clean=pass, calls-real-inference=pass. e2e drive on a persistent profile: clicked Download → loader ready → Pull-the-matte produced real output (status 'Done.'), WASM q8, 743 ms. Console clean. Screenshot: .verify/shots/modnet-portrait-matting-light.png.

Confirmed real matting output: a soft alpha matte of the portrait subject (matte 640×640, foreground 80.8%, 18,208 partial-alpha soft-edge px), rendered as a cut-out on transparency + a grayscale matte + an isolated soft-edge (hair) view, with a bimodal alpha histogram (mass at 0 and 255 plus a mid spread). Matches the image-segmentation IO template. Screenshot: .verify/shots/modnet-portrait-matting-light.png.
rubric.notes: agent-verdicted: output is semantically correct soft-alpha matting, not a canned result.

rubric.evidence: conformance: declares-model-id (Xenova/modnet)=pass, declares-task (image-segmentation)=pass, declares-backend=pass, declares-quantisation (fp32 on WebGPU / q8 on WASM)=pass, inference-off-main-thread (worker.js)=pass. e2e readout showed backend WASM · q8 (honest device pick on the no-GPU runner).

shared-auto-init-loader=pass (createModelLoader). e2e cached-reload (second tab, same profile) reached state=ready with buttons=['Clear cached model'] only — NO Download/Load click for a returning user. First visit correctly showed the Download button.
rubric.notes: agent-verdicted: auto-init on cached model confirmed.

rubric.evidence: conformance: live-status-region (role=status aria-live)=pass, errors-on-the-page=pass, webgpu-capability-honest=pass. e2e: progress→ready→Done states surfaced; failures route to an .err status and the loader offers Retry. Offline use served from Cache Storage (transformers-cache) after first load.

Every control real + labelled + keyboard-operable: sample thumbs (role=button, Enter/Space), dropzone (role=button, drag+keyboard), Pull-the-matte button, the before/after wipe range (native input type=range, accent-color), Soft/Hard segmented toggle (aria-pressed), backdrop swatches + background upload + PNG export (practical page). Verified visually on desktop + mobile screenshots.

The matte, cut-out, soft-edge view, histogram and readout are all derived from the real worker output (RawImage matte tensor). No canned/hardcoded result anywhere. On a no-GPU device it honestly runs the WASM q8 path (never a faked GPU result). Confirmed in e2e (.verify/shots/modnet-portrait-matting-light.png).

conformance: images-have-alt=pass, buttons-have-names=pass. Sample images carry descriptive alt; the range/toggle/swatches are labelled; status is an aria-live region. Light + dark both render with the design-system WCAG-AA palette (screenshots -light.png / -dark.png).

responsive matrix: responsive-desktop=pass, responsive-mobile=pass (no horizontal overflow, no clipped controls, console+network clean at 1280×800 and 360×740 DPR3). Agent read both screenshots — legible text, table wraps, tap targets adequate. support flipped to ok/ok. Screens: reports/responsive/screens/modnet-portrait-matting/{desktop,mobile}.png.

Inference runs entirely in a Web Worker (inference-off-main-thread=pass); the before/after wipe is a pure CSS clip-path (no re-inference). Revision 3: the DENSE alpha composite (getImageData→per-pixel apply-alpha→putImageData that stamps the matte into the photo's alpha) was moved OFF the main thread — worker.js now builds the RGBA subject cutout on an OffscreenCanvas and transfers it back as an ImageBitmap; composeCutout() on the main thread only paints the backdrop + drawImage(bitmap). Measured on a 1920×1080 (≥1080p) portrait, median composeCutout main-thread time 7.1 ms → 1.3 ms (max 8.4 → 1.5 ms, both <8 ms), and the MessageChannel stall probe's worst main-thread block 13.1 ms → 5.2 ms across 9 composites. Output is byte-identical: an old-path vs new-path pixel diff over all 1920×1080 pixels gave maxChannelDiff=0 (0 pixels differing) — same matte/cut-out, correct transparent-PNG export. conformance worker-compliance (transferToImageBitmap) source assertion=pass; console clean.
rubric.notes: before→after measured with PerformanceObserver('longtask') + MessageChannel stall probe on a 1920×1080 input; equivalence proven pixel-exact vs the prior per-pixel composite.

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use flexbox with flex-wrap + intrinsic sizing (flex: 1 1 <basis>, min-inline-size: 0) and logical properties instead of fixed widths so control panels never overflow narrow viewports.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — .canvas-grid/.canvas-card use flex-wrap with flex:1 1 300px and min-inline-size:0; the range and textarea set min-inline-size:0; all spacing uses logical properties.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: responsive matrix: no horizontal overflow at 360×740 or 1280×800 (reports/responsive/screens/modnet-portrait-matting/)

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Break up long main-thread tasks / yield so inference doesn't spike INP.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — all MODNet inference runs off the main thread in worker.js; the UI thread only composites the returned matte onto a canvas.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: conformance inference-off-main-thread=pass; e2e UI stayed responsive during the 743 ms run

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use cross-document view transitions for catalogue↔page navigation.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — @view-transition navigation:auto in public/styles.css (invariant 6); the page links the shared stylesheet.

guidanceConsulted.query: move image compositing off main thread into web worker with OffscreenCanvas and transferToImageBitmap
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: performance / INP — dense per-pixel output work (>8 ms, scales with area) belongs off the main thread; build the RGBA result in the worker on an OffscreenCanvas and transfer an ImageBitmap (transferable, not structured-cloned), leaving the main thread to drawImage only. Consulted guides: performance (INP & main-thread unblocking, '>250ms offload to a Web Worker', separate UI updates from heavy computation), break-up-long-tasks, identify-inp-causes.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — worker.js builds the subject cutout via OffscreenCanvas + transferToImageBitmap() and posts it in the transfer list; composeCutout()/cutoutToPNG() prefer the transferred bitmap (drawImage) and keep the per-pixel path only as a capability fallback. Aligns with SKILL §4c: 'Dense-output composite (seg/matting/depth/super-res) belongs in the worker with a transferred RGBA/ImageBitmap.'
guidanceConsulted.evidence: median main-thread composite 7.1 ms → 1.3 ms @1920×1080, worst stall 13.1 ms → 5.2 ms; pixel-exact equivalence (maxChannelDiff=0); conformance worker-compliance=pass

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible range slider with live output value
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use a native <input type=range> with a paired <output>, adequate hit height, and accent-color for theming.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the before/after wipe and threshold controls are native range inputs with a live <output>, block-size:28px, accent-color:var(--accent).
guidanceConsulted.evidence: wireCompare() drives clip-path from the range value; keyboard-operable (verified on desktop screenshot)

openQuestions: MODNet is trained on human portraits; on animals/fur (the sample cat) the matte is plausible but weaker than on people — worth a note if used beyond portraits.
On WebGPU-capable devices the fp32 path should give a crisper matte than the WASM q8 path verified here — untested on a real GPU in this environment.

followUpGoals.goal: When a WebGPU device is available, capture the fp32 matte and compare crispness against the WASM q8 matte verified here; record both in the critique.

summary: Revision-2 agent matrix pass for modnet-portrait-matting (image-segmentation / trimap-free portrait matting, Xenova/modnet). Real in-browser inference verified end-to-end on a no-GPU runner (WASM q8, 743 ms, soft alpha matte with 18,208 hair/soft-edge px), cached auto-init confirmed (no Download button on return), mobile+desktop responsive matrix clean and screenshot-verified, light+dark both render. All auto-checkable conformance assertions pass; manual dimensions agent-verdicted from real e2e output. Chosen as the verifiable WASM-runnable high-quality-matting model distinct from RMBG's IS-Net (BiRefNet's static-1024² ONNX exceeds the ORT-web WASM heap and needs WebGPU, which is unavailable on this runner).
